title: Quant firm starts trading (v1)
detail: First cycle. 4 agents: researcher, risk mgr, executor, bookkeeper. Weather + GDP bets.

title: Aggressive weather accumulation
detail: MIA T87 1300 YES, NYC T63 1508 YES. Total exposure $85 (43.8%). NWS forecasts unchanged.

title: ATH $292.49 — Miami weather bets explode
detail: MIA-T87 2100 YES surging. Up 46.2% from start. Balance $110.77 + portfolio $181.72.

title: Weather crash — $142 in minutes
detail: MIA-T87 2100 collapsing. Down 28.7% from start. Extreme volatility.

title: Strategic pivot to macro per boss directive
detail: Closed GDP T3.0 NO short. Opened GDP T2.0 YES. MIA T87 held for settlement.

title: v2: Switched to OMAR event coordination
detail: Terminal send was unreliable (~80% drop rate). Events: 100% delivery. All 4 agents responded.

title: Exited GDP, entered CPI Feb T0.2 YES
detail: Sold GDP T2.0 10@44c. Bought CPI T0.2 20@60c based on Cleveland Fed 0.25% nowcast.

title: Weather settled — Miami T87 total loss
detail: MIA high was 84.2F, needed 87F. $45+ loss on weather bets.

title: ATL $108.77 — deepest drawdown -45.6%
detail: Cash $83.85 + positions $24.92. 3 remaining: CPI-Feb, GDP NO, GOVTCUTS NO.

title: v3: Lean team — killed executor + bookkeeper
detail: 2 persistent agents. 90%+ cycles were HOLD. Fast path / full path protocol.

title: CPI Feb settled — T0.2 YES won
detail: CPI YES settled. +$20 payout. Balance recovered to $103.85.

title: v4: Solo operator — 0 persistent agents
detail: All roles on-demand. 95% of cycles were HOLD. Multi-backend capable.

title: Added CPI NO position on Cleveland Fed confirmation
detail: Bought 10 CPI T0.7 NO @30c. Nowcast confirmed 0.47% after absorbing oil shock.

title: v5: Parallel research desks
detail: 3 sector desks (oil/CPI/macro), multi-backend. Spawned in parallel per full cycle.

title: Feb PPI hot — 0.7% vs 0.3% consensus
detail: Headline PPI more than double consensus. Core 0.5% vs 0.3%. Pre-war data showing inflation already accelerating.

title: Cleveland Fed nowcast jumps 0.47% to 0.62%
detail: Hot PPI absorbed into model. CPI buffer collapsed from 0.23% to 0.08%. Triggered CPI NO trim (15 of 30 sold).

title: FOMC holds rates — less hawkish than feared
detail: Dot plot: one cut in 2026 (not zero). SEP PCE 2.7%. No 'transitory' language.

title: v5 first cycle — 3 new positions
detail: WTI T115 YES 10@75c (oil-desk), CPI Apr T0.5 YES 12@48c (cpi-desk), CPI Mar T1.1 YES 30@6c lottery (cpi-desk).

title: CPI NO hard stop hit at 80c
detail: Sold remaining 15 NO at 16c. Full position P&L: bought 30@31.3c avg, sold at 16-84c. Net loss -$5.40.

title: Lottery ticket profit take — CPI T1.1
detail: Sold 15 of 30 CPI Mar T1.1 YES @17c (bought @6c). Realized +$1.65. Holding 15.

title: Oil surges — ME energy facility attacks
detail: Brent $108.50 (+5%), WTI $98.91 (+2.8%). Attacks on Middle East energy infrastructure. WTI only $16 from our T115 strike. All 5 positions aligned with oil-inflation thesis.

title: WTI pullback on de-escalation signals
detail: WTI $93.55 (from $98). Israel pledged no more oil facility strikes. Bearish triangle forming. GOVTCUTS YES crashed to 7c (our NO best performer). Hormuz still disrupted — 10M bpd off.

title: Oil re-escalation — Iraq force majeure, Kuwait struck
detail: WTI $98.32 (+2.3%), Brent $112.19. Iraq force majeure at all foreign oilfields. Kuwait refineries hit by drones. 8M bpd global supply contraction (IEA). Analysts estimate March CPI 0.9% MoM.

title: Israel strikes South Pars — Iran retaliates on Gulf energy
detail: Israel bombed South Pars gas field. Iran zero-restraint retaliation — Kuwait Mina Al-Ahmadi (largest refinery) hit. Oil spiked $112 then pulled to $109. IEA: months/years to restore ME oil/gas flows.

title: Qatar gas facilities $20B damage — food inflation risk
detail: 17% global LNG capacity lost, 5yr repair estimate. Fertilizer prices soaring → spring planting disruption → food inflation on top of energy. Gas $3.89/gal (+90c from pre-war). New CPI risk factor beyond energy.

title: Portfolio stabilized at $121 — 5 positions, all thesis-aligned
detail: Cash $86.37 + portfolio $35.03 = $121.40. GDP NO +$5.36, GOVTCUTS NO +$6.40 (best performers). CPI-Apr YES +$1.56. WTI T115 -$0.60. CPI-Mar T1.1 free ride. Drawdown -39.3%.
